summary: Write guru.db schema (nodes, edges, staged_tags, staged_edges, staged_concepts)
description:
## Goal

Define the SQLite schema for `data/guru.db` covering live graph tables and Stage 3 staging tables.

## Tables

**Live graph:**
- `nodes (id, type, tradition_id?, label, definition?, metadata_json)` — type ∈ {tradition, concept, chunk}
- `edges (id, source_id, target_id, type, tier, justification, created_at)` — type ∈ {BELONGS_TO, EXPRESSES, PARALLELS, CONTRASTS, DERIVES_FROM}; tier ∈ {verified, proposed, inferred}

**Staging (per docs/guru-implementation.md §"Stage 3"):**
- `staged_tags (id, chunk_id, concept_id, score, justification, is_new_concept, new_concept_def, status, reviewed_by, reviewed_at)`
- `staged_edges (id, source_concept, target_concept, edge_type, confidence, justification, source_chunk, target_chunk, status, tier, reviewed_by, reviewed_at)`
- `staged_concepts (id, proposed_id, definition, motivating_chunk, status, reviewed_by, reviewed_at)`

**Bookkeeping:**
- `tagging_progress (chunk_id, completed_at)` — supports `--resume` on `tag_concepts.py`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Schema is checked into the repo as a `.sql` file or Python module
- All foreign keys and indexes for hot lookups (chunk_id, concept_id, status) are declared
- Compatible with downstream scripts (`graph_bootstrap.py`, `tag_concepts.py`, `review_*.py`, `propose_edges.py`)

resolution.note: scripts/schema.sql: nodes, edges, staged_tags, staged_edges, staged_concepts, tagging_progress. All indexes, FK constraints, CHECK constraints. Applied to fresh data/guru.db — 6 tables confirmed.
